title: Add Party/Buyer GST & Address to Version 24 UI with Invoice Display

requirements.summary: Restore the full Version 24 UI exactly as it was, including the company logo, layout, color scheme, navigation, and all existing components without altering any existing design or functionality
acceptanceCriteria: All Version 24 pages render identically to Version 24 (summary, add-bill, search, parties, company-report, settings, invoice print)
Company logo appears in the header/invoice exactly as in Version 24
No existing Version 24 UI elements are removed or repositioned

file_operations.description: Verify and restore the company logo in the header using the generated asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/manish-infratech-logo.dim_320x80.png. Ensure the logo placement, size, and styling match Version 24 exactly. The header should maintain its red background and navigation links without any layout changes.

Verify and restore the company logo in the invoice print view using the generated asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/manish-infratech-logo.dim_320x80.png or frontend/public/assets/generated/mi-logo.dim_128x128.png. Ensure the logo appears in the invoice header exactly as in Version 24 and is visible both on screen and when printing.

Verify that all Version 24 color scheme variables, theme definitions, and print styles remain intact. Ensure no CSS changes have altered the visual appearance of existing components. Maintain the OKLCH-based color system and print-specific styles that hide UI chrome during invoice printing.

Verify that all Version 24 Tailwind configuration including custom colors (navy/brand palettes), fonts (Inter, Poppins), border radius tokens, and shadow utilities remain unchanged. Ensure no theme modifications have been introduced that would affect the existing UI appearance.

requirements.summary: Add party/buyer input fields (GST Number and Address) to AddBillForm and EditBillModal with auto-fill from party profile, displayed in a clearly labeled 'Bill To / Party Details' section
acceptanceCriteria: AddBillForm displays Party GST Number and Party Address input fields
EditBillModal displays Party GST Number and Party Address input fields pre-populated with saved values
When a party is selected via autocomplete, partyGstNumber and partyAddress auto-fill from the party's stored profile
Fields are editable and their values are submitted with the bill

file_operations.description: Add a 'Bill To / Party Details' section with two new input fields: Party GST Number (text input) and Party Address (textarea). Position this section logically near the party name autocomplete field without disrupting the Version 24 layout. Implement auto-fill logic: when a party is selected via the existing autocomplete, fetch the party profile using the getPartyProfile backend capability and populate partyGstNumber and partyAddress fields from the returned PartyProfile.gstNumber and PartyProfile.address. Allow manual editing of these fields. Update the form submission to include partyGstNumber and partyAddress in the BillOperation data sent to the addBill backend capability. Ensure the form state and validation accommodate these new optional fields.

Add a 'Bill To / Party Details' section with two new input fields: Party GST Number (text input) and Party Address (textarea). Pre-populate these fields with the existing bill's partyGstNo (mapped from backend Bill.partyGstNo optional field) and a corresponding address field when the modal opens. Implement auto-fill logic: if the user changes the party name via autocomplete, fetch the new party's profile using getPartyProfile and update the GST and address fields accordingly. Allow manual editing. Update the form submission to include the edited partyGstNumber and partyAddress in the updatedBillOp sent to the editBill backend capability. Handle backward compatibility: if partyGstNo is undefined on the bill, default to an empty string.

Add a React Query hook useGetPartyProfile(partyName: string) that calls actor.getPartyProfile(partyName) to fetch the PartyProfile containing gstNumber and address. Use this hook in AddBillForm and EditBillModal to auto-fill party GST and address fields when a party is selected. Ensure the query is enabled only when a valid partyName is provided and the actor is available.

requirements.summary: Display the party/buyer's name, GST number, and address in the InvoicePrintView component in a 'Bill To' section, visible on screen and when printing
acceptanceCriteria: InvoicePrintView shows a 'Bill To' section with party name, GST number (labeled 'GST No:'), and address
The 'Bill To' section is visible on the invoice screen view
The 'Bill To' section is visible and correctly formatted when the invoice is printed or saved as PDF
If partyGstNumber or partyAddress is empty, the section gracefully omits or hides those sub-fields

file_operations.description: Add a 'Bill To' section that displays the party/buyer details: party name (from bill.partyName), GST number (from bill.partyGstNo with label 'GST No:'), and address (from a new partyAddress field that should be added to the Bill type rendering). Position this section prominently near the top of the invoice, typically opposite or below the company/seller details section. Use conditional rendering: if bill.partyGstNo is undefined or empty, omit the GST No line; similarly handle the address field. Ensure the section styling is consistent with Version 24 invoice formatting and that it renders correctly both on screen and in print/PDF mode. Verify that the print-specific CSS from index.css applies to this section.

requirements.summary: Display the party/buyer's GST number in SummaryPage bills table, SearchBillsPage results table, and PartySummaryPage
acceptanceCriteria: SummaryPage bills table includes party GST number visible per bill row
SearchBillsPage results table shows party GST number per bill row
PartySummaryPage displays party GST number next to each party name

file_operations.description: Add a new column 'Party GST' to the bills table that displays bill.partyGstNo for each bill row. Position this column logically near the party name column. Use conditional rendering to display the GST number if present, or show a placeholder (e.g., '—' or 'N/A') if bill.partyGstNo is undefined or empty. Ensure the column width and styling fit within the existing Version 24 table layout without breaking responsive behavior. This component is used by both SummaryPage and SearchBillsPage, so this change will cover both requirements.

Add a visible display of the party GST number alongside each party name in the party summary list or table. The PartySummary data type from the backend already includes a gstNumber field. Render this field in the UI for each party entry, labeled appropriately (e.g., 'GST No:' or as a column header if using a table layout). Ensure the GST number is visible without requiring inline editing to be activated. Maintain the Version 24 styling and layout; position the GST number logically near the party name or billing statistics.
